item.title: MCP is dead. Long live the CLI
item.description: I’m going to make a bold claim: MCP is already dying. We may not fully realize it yet, but the signs are there. OpenClaw doesn’t support it. Pi doesn’t support it. And for good reason.

item.title: Killing my inner Necron
item.description: On surviving surgery, confronting mortality, and finding peace on the other side.

item.title: Dating Apps, Data Structures, and Dopamine | ErrorCode0
item.description: A tale of app building.

item.title: Having fun with modern web APIs
item.description: One of the many reasons why I wanted to start blogging again was to explore modern browser APIs. Technically, I work as a full stack developer for my day job, but I usually spend most of my time on t…

item.title: Blog by Dario
item.description: Blog - Senior Mobile Engineer - Dario Milicic

item.title: Background Jobs for TanStack Start with pg-boss by Jamie Davenport
item.description: TanStack Start server functions are great for request/response logic, but some work shouldn't block the user. This post walks through wiring pg-boss into a TanStack Start app to handle durable background jobs — with a typed registry, a Nitro plugin, and a fan-out pattern for post-signup flows.

item.title: Sit On Your Ass Web Development
item.description: Writing about the big beautiful mess that is making things for the world wide web.

item.title: What I learned building a Multi-Agent System
item.description: Building a multi-agent AI system to automate cloud assessment documentation. Learn about agent design, role specialization, MCP servers, and context engineering for complex LLM workflows.

item.title: The Colour of Production - Leon Mika
item.description: On the colour-coding system used for different development environments.

item.title: You think unemployed, I think retired
item.description:
Work sucks, I know
- blink-182

item.title: Who Actually Owns Your ATProto Identity? Hint: It's Probably Not You
item.description: ATProto gives your PDS operator full control of your signing and rotation keys, letting them impersonate you across every app in the ecosystem or kill

item.title: Let's Talk About the Humanoid Robot in the Room
item.description: General purpose humanoid robots are like self-driving, but actually much harder.

item.title: Software for One | Koen van Gilst
item.description: Instead of downloading someone else's app, I built exactly what I wanted for less than a dollar.

item.title: Mutability considered harmful
item.description: We live in a constantly changing world. Updates happen all the time. It's counter-intuitive to say that we need to somehow avoid mutabilit...

item.title: Expert Beginners and Lone Wolves will dominate this early LLM era
item.description: After migrating this blog from a static site generator into Drupal in 2009, I noted: As a sad side-effect, all the blog comments are gone. Forever. Wiped out. But have no fear, we can start new discussions on many new posts! I archived all the comments from the old 'Thingamablog' version of the blog, but can't repost them here (at least, not with my time constraints... it would just take a nice import script, but I don't have the time for that now).

item.title: Service health - Mar 01, 2026 | AWS Health Dashboard
item.description: View the overall status and health of AWS services using the AWS Health Dashboard.

item.title: The looming AI clownpocalypse · honnibal.dev
item.description: Exploits will soon be cheaper to develop autonomously than they earn. What then?

item.title: Ghostty Docs
item.description: Ghostty is a fast, feature-rich, and cross-platform terminal emulator that uses platform-native UI and GPU acceleration.

item.title: Ad-Supported AI Chat Demo — See Every Ad Type in Action
item.description: Experience what AI chat looks like with heavy advertising: banners, interstitials, sponsored responses, freemium gates, and more.

item.title: Decision Trees
item.description: An introduction to the Decision Trees, Entropy, and Information Gain.

item.title: AI Made Writing Code Easier. It Made Engineering Harder.
item.description: Writing code is easier than ever. Being a software engineer is harder than ever. The paradox nobody talks about, and what engineers and leaders should do.

item.title: Switch to Claude without starting over | Claude
item.description: Transfer your preferences, projects, and context from other AI providers into Claude. Switch without losing what makes your AI useful.

item.title: Our agreement with the Department of War
item.description: Details on OpenAI’s contract with the Department of War, outlining safety red lines, legal protections, and how AI systems will be deployed in classified environments.
